name: rate-limits
context: GitHub API rate limits — REST, GraphQL, search, secondary limits, and abuse detection
info: GitHub enforces rate limits on all API interactions to ensure fair usage and platform stability. Authenticated REST requests are limited to 5,000/hour, GraphQL to 5,000 points/hour, and search to 30 requests/minute. GitHub Apps get 5,000/hour per installation. Secondary rate limits protect against burst traffic and abuse patterns. Conditional requests (ETag/If-None-Match) do not count against limits. Monitor remaining quota via response headers and the GraphQL `rateLimit` field. This reference covers all rate limit tiers, headers, strategies for staying within limits, and handling 403/429 responses. See [[github/rest]] and [[github/graphql]] for API conventions, and [[github/auth]] for how authentication method affects your quota.
triggers: how many api calls can I make

sections.title: Primary rate limits
sections.body: REST: 5,000 requests/hour authenticated, 60/hour unauthenticated. GraphQL: 5,000 points/hour (authentication required). Search: 30 requests/minute authenticated, 10/minute unauthenticated. GitHub Apps: 5,000/hour per installation. Limits reset hourly; check the `X-RateLimit-Reset` header for the exact reset timestamp.

sections.title: Secondary (abuse) rate limits
sections.body: Secondary limits prevent burst traffic: max 90 concurrent requests, 900 GraphQL points/minute, 80 content-creating requests/minute, and 1 request/second per endpoint for polling. Violations return 403 or 429 with `Retry-After`. These limits are not publicly enumerated per-endpoint and can change. Always implement retry logic.

sections.title: GraphQL point calculation
sections.body: Each GraphQL query costs points based on node count: the sum of all `first`/`last` arguments across connections. Nested connections multiply. Query the `rateLimit` field to see the `cost` of any query before it runs at scale. Keep `first` values low in nested connections to avoid expensive queries.

sections.title: Handling and retry strategies
sections.body: Use `@octokit/plugin-throttling` for automatic backoff and retry. Parse `Retry-After` headers for 429 responses. Implement exponential backoff with jitter for custom clients. Use conditional requests (ETag/If-None-Match) to avoid consuming quota. Use webhooks instead of polling. Cache responses locally.
